meta_title: For Students: SaaS pricing to Solve can't debug code (Advanced Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome can't debug code. A Advanced-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: SaaS Pricing Strategy: Master Debugging & Scale Your Projects
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with debugging. Master SaaS pricing and AI-driven development with Antigravity and Claude Code to build professional-grade apps as a student.
content.answer_block: When your code breaks, don't panic. Use AI agents like Claude Code to isolate logic errors and Antigravity to map system dependencies. By integrating a robust SaaS pricing model early, you transition from a coder to a product architect, creating sustainable value that solves real-world user problems while building your portfolio.
content.intro:
The screen flickers. You’ve been staring at a stack trace for three hours, and the console error remains a cryptic, taunting void. You aren't just failing to debug; you're hitting the wall where theory meets the brutal reality of production-grade software. This is the moment most students quit—but it's also the threshold of becoming a senior developer.

True engineering isn't just writing code; it's architecting systems that sustain themselves. By mastering AI-assisted debugging and implementing professional SaaS pricing models, you stop being a student who writes scripts and become a builder who launches businesses. Let’s turn that frustration into your greatest competitive advantage.

steps.title: Architecting the Pricing Model
steps.description: Before fixing bugs, identify the value of your features. Define usage-based vs. flat-tier pricing to ensure your code supports the billing logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current codebase and suggest a Stripe-compatible billing schema that maps to my existing user feature set.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Review my schema.prisma and suggest 3 pricing tiers based on usage limits'

steps.title: Isolating Logic Leaks
steps.description: Use Claude Code to trace the specific function causing your runtime error. We need to identify if the bug is in the business logic or the API integration.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Trace the execution path for this error: [Paste Error]. Identify the exact line where the state becomes inconsistent.

steps.title: Automating Unit Tests
steps.description: Don't debug by hand. Generate a test suite that forces the bug to reveal itself, ensuring your pricing logic remains sound under load.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Jest test suite that mocks the payment gateway and validates the user subscription status logic.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Create a test file for the pricing service that covers edge cases for failed payments'

steps.title: Refactoring for Scalability
steps.description: Clean up the mess. Use AI to refactor the code that caused the bug into a modular service, making it easier to maintain as your user base grows.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor this module into a clean, testable service pattern. Remove hardcoded logic and inject it as a configuration.

steps.title: Deploying with Confidence
steps.description: Finalize your production environment. Ensure the billing hooks are secure and the error tracking is active.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Audit my production deployment config for security vulnerabilities and missing error handling blocks.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'npm run build && npm run test'

pro_tips: Always treat your pricing model as code; move billing logic into a separate service to avoid polluting your UI components.
Use Claude Code to generate 'self-healing' scripts that auto-restart services when specific memory thresholds are hit.
When debugging, ask the AI to 'explain the state transition' rather than just 'fix the code' to deepen your understanding.
Implement feature flags in your SaaS pricing tiers so you can disable buggy features without rolling back the entire deployment.
Document your debugging process as a 'Post-Mortem' in your GitHub repo—employers value this more than a perfect commit history.
content.next_steps: Once your app is stable, deploy it to a free-tier cloud provider like Vercel or Railway and share it on the AI Builder community forum for a code review.
content.conversion_block: You are now equipped to handle complex architectural challenges and monetize your work. Don't let a single error stop your momentum. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
